title: Still We Sail
tagline: Carrying grief, love and memory across unknown waters
aesthetic: Atlantic noir — deep blue, salt white, muted gold

artwork.gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 70%, #0d2040 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 75% 40%, #0a1828 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 90%, #162845 0%, transparent 40%), linear-gradient(180deg, #020408 0%, #070e1a 35%, #0d1a2e 65%, #060d18 100%)
artwork.description: A dark shoreline at the edge of night. The water barely visible beneath layers of mist and departure.
artwork.mood: The colour of something you cannot name but will carry forever.

clues.sentence: We did not choose to leave the shore. The shore left ____.
clues.hint: It was not a choice we made.

fragment.text: The last moment on land is always ordinary. You don't know it's the last one until you're already gone.

clues.sentence: Grief is love with nowhere ____.
clues.hint: Love that has lost its direction.

fragment.text: There is no graceful way to begin an exile. You simply step forward, and keep walking until the ground beneath you turns to water.

clues.sentence: The sea does not ask permission. It takes what we ____.
clues.hint: What we grip tightest.

fragment.text: Not every wound is visible. Some are shaped like an open horizon.

clues.sentence: Departure is never a single moment. It is a wound that ____.
clues.hint: Something that does not go away.

fragment.text: The ones who leave carry the weight of both worlds — the one abandoned, and the one not yet found.

clues.sentence: Arrange these words to complete the lyric.
clues.hint: Start with 'the'.

fragment.text: The sea keeps our names long after we forget them.

clues.sentence: "We carry the shore in our bones, salt and all." This lyric speaks to:
clues.hint: Think of what salt and bones represent emotionally.

fragment.text: Grief is not what we leave behind. It is what we carry forward.

completionFragment.text: This is where the atlas begins. Not with a destination — but with a shore that grew smaller behind you, until it was only light, and then nothing, and then the sea.

artwork.gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 60%, #0f2540 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 80% 30%, #0a1f38 0%, transparent 45%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 80%, #1a2f45 0%, transparent 35%), radial-gradient(circle at 60% 20%, #d4dde31a 0%, transparent 30%), linear-gradient(165deg, #030710 0%, #08111e 40%, #0f1d30 70%, #050a14 100%)
artwork.description: Dark ocean blues with salt-white mist suspended in air. The surface of something very old.
artwork.mood: The texture of everything you never said, preserved.

clues.sentence: Salt preserves everything it touches. This is why the ocean never ____.
clues.hint: What the sea does with all it has witnessed.

fragment.text: Salt is the oldest memory. The ocean has been holding the same grief since the beginning of water.

clues.sentence: What cannot be said is carried in the body like water between ____.
clues.hint: The structure inside us.

fragment.text: Some things cannot be translated. They can only be lived, then carried — silent — in the body.

clues.sentence: Memory is not a place. It is a ____.
clues.hint: Something that comes and goes without asking.

fragment.text: The tide comes in. The tide goes out. Memory works the same way — but never on schedule.

clues.sentence: I have learned to read the sea the way the blind read ____.
clues.hint: How we know someone without seeing them.

fragment.text: You learn to read the faces of strangers like a navigator reads currents — searching for the familiar inside the unfamiliar.

completionFragment.text: The sea keeps everything. Not as objects — but as pressure. As temperature. As the way light bends differently here than it did at home.

artwork.gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 55%, #1a0e04 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 25% 70%, #241505 0%, transparent 45%), radial-gradient(circle at 75% 30%, #c4953a1a 0%, transparent 35%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 60% 80%, #180b03 0%, transparent 40%), linear-gradient(180deg, #060304 0%, #0f0805 40%, #180d06 65%, #060304 100%)
artwork.description: Warm amber darkness. The colour of old wood and breath and a room where music was played once and never forgotten.
artwork.mood: This is what saudade looks like when it has found a shape.

clues.sentence: The accordion breathes where words cannot ____.
clues.hint: The furthest place language fails to find.

fragment.text: Before there were words for this feeling, there was the accordion. Breath in. Breath out. The bellows open. A whole country fits inside.

clues.sentence: Saudade is not sadness. It is the ache of beauty ____.
clues.hint: When beauty has already happened.

fragment.text: Saudade is the word the sea invented for itself. The longing of water for the shore it has already passed.

clues.sentence: Music does not heal wounds. It teaches wounds to ____.
clues.hint: What a voice does with pain.

fragment.text: Every wound that heals into music was once a wound that had nowhere else to go.

clues.sentence: The bellows open. Somewhere, someone who is gone ____.
clues.hint: What the music makes possible.

fragment.text: When she played, those who had never left could almost understand what it meant to have lost something that was still there.

completionFragment.text: The accordion does not perform grief. It inhabits it. Every note is breath — and breath is the oldest proof that something is still alive.

artwork.gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 45%, #0a1525 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 50%, #06101e 0%, transparent 70%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 30%, #b8c5d00a 0%, transparent 40%), linear-gradient(180deg, #020406 0%, #040b16 30%, #08121e 55%, #040b16 80%, #020406 100%)
artwork.description: Near-total darkness. A single silver thread of moonlight on water — the only proof there is a surface at all.
artwork.mood: The colour of trusting something you cannot see.

clues.sentence: In the middle of the crossing, there is no shore in either ____.
clues.hint: What you look toward when lost.

fragment.text: The hardest crossing is not the longest one. It is the one where you cannot see either shore.

clues.sentence: Trust is not certainty. It is continuing to move through ____.
clues.hint: What surrounds you when you cannot see.

fragment.text: You learn to trust the dark the same way you learned to trust the sea — not because it promised anything, but because there was no other direction.

clues.sentence: The stars cannot tell you where you are. Only that you are not ____.
clues.hint: What the stars disprove.

fragment.text: The stars are not a map. They are a reminder: you are still here, still moving. Tonight, that is enough.

clues.sentence: Night does not end because you ask it to. It ends because you ____.
clues.hint: What you did when you wanted to stop.

fragment.text: Night does not defeat those who have already survived something darker.

completionFragment.text: The crossing is almost done. Not because the sea ends — but because you kept moving through the part where it seemed impossible to move.

artwork.gradient: radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 75%, #c4953a22 0%, transparent 50%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 60%, #1b3a4b 0%, transparent 65%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 30% 40%, #0d1b2a 0%, transparent 45%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 70% 30%, #0a1525 0%, transparent 40%), linear-gradient(180deg, #030610 0%, #0a1628 35%, #162845 60%, #0a1628 80%, #030610 100%)
artwork.description: Deep indigo sea and a golden horizon that was not there before — appearing slowly, as if the light itself had been waiting.
artwork.mood: The colour of choosing to continue.

clues.sentence: After grief, after distance, after everything we lost — still we ____.
clues.hint: What the album is named for.

fragment.text: The ones who keep moving are not always brave. Sometimes they are simply carrying love across water.

clues.sentence: We are not heroes of this crossing. We are simply ____.
clues.hint: Not heroism — something quieter.

fragment.text: We are not heroes of this story. We are the water itself — shaped by everything it has passed through, still moving forward.

clues.sentence: The atlas is not a map of places. It is a map of ____.
clues.hint: What this atlas holds.

fragment.text: This atlas is not complete. It was never meant to be. A memory atlas grows with every crossing.

clues.sentence: What we carry becomes who we ____.
clues.hint: Our identity, formed by weight.

fragment.text: What we carry becomes who we are. What we sail toward becomes what we leave behind.

clues.sentence: We sail not because the sea is safe. We sail because the sea is ____.
clues.hint: What belongs to those who dare it.

fragment.text: Still we sail. Not because the sea ends. But because the sailing is the answer.

completionFragment.text: You have crossed the Still We Sail memory. All five memories unlocked. All shards collected. The atlas grows. The sea continues. So do you.
